gtfs._comment: GTFS transit feed discovery and caching. Feeds are auto-discovered from the Mobility Database catalog by intersecting feed bounding boxes with the configured region counties
gtfs._catalog_url_help: URL to the Mobility Database CSV catalog of GTFS feeds worldwide
gtfs._catalog_max_age_days_help: Re-download the catalog CSV if the local copy is older than this many days
gtfs._feed_max_age_days_help: Re-download individual GTFS feed zips if the cached copy is older than this many days
gtfs._cache_dir_help: Local directory for cached catalog CSV and extracted GTFS feed folders (e.g., data/gtfs/{feed_id}/)
gtfs._country_filter_help: Only consider feeds from this country code when filtering the catalog

api_keys._help: API keys for GTFS feeds that require authentication, keyed by domain. Register free at https://developer.wmata.com/

modes._comment: Transportation modes. Each key is a mode name. Set 'enabled': false to disable a mode without removing its config. Modes are flat — bus and rail are separate entries, not sub-modes of 'pt'
modes._enabled_help: Set to false to disable this mode. Disabled modes are excluded from mode choice, availability checking, GTFS setup, and MATSim transit config. Default is true if omitted
modes._matsim_mode_help: The mode string written to MATSim plans.xml legs. Multiple modes can map to the same MATSim mode (e.g., bus and rail both map to 'pt')
modes._availability_help: How to determine if a mode is available for a given OD pair. String 'universal' = always available. Object with 'type' key: 'gtfs' = available if GTFS stops exist near both origin and destination, 'distance' = available if OD distance is within max, 'zone_list' = available if both locations are in specified zones
modes._survey_rate_help: Mode share rate from survey data. 'auto' = compute from TBI/NHTS survey data, or specify a float (e.g., 0.85 for 85%)
modes._config_rate_help: Target mode share rate for blending with survey rate. null = no blending (use survey rate only)
modes._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending survey_rate and config_rate: 0.0 = pure survey rate, 1.0 = pure config rate, 0.5 = average both
modes._share_adjustment_help: Additive adjustment to the final mode share in percentage points (e.g., 0.05 adds 5%, -0.1 subtracts 10%). Applied after blending

availability._route_types_help: GTFS route_type codes: 3=Bus, 11=Trolleybus. See GTFS spec for full list
availability._access_buffer_meters_help: LA Metro bus network is less dense than Chicago but still extensive along major corridors; 900m reflects realistic walking catchment for LA's grid-arterial bus routes

bus._config_rate_help: LA bus share target. ACS shows ~4-5% bus commute in LA County; non-work trips are lower. Set to 0.09 pre-filtering; chain_consistency intersection will reduce to ~3-4% in output
bus._blend_weight_help: Lean heavily on config_rate since NHTS national bus share (~2%) is below LA's actual usage

availability._access_buffer_meters_help: LA Metro Rail stations (Red/Purple/Blue/Expo/Gold/Green lines) and Metrolink stops are spaced 1-2km apart; 1500m captures realistic catchment

rail._config_rate_help: LA rail share target. Metro Rail carries ~40% of LA transit riders; set to 0.06 pre-filtering; chain_consistency will reduce to ~2-3% in output
rail._blend_weight_help: Lean heavily on config_rate since NHTS national rail share (~1%) is below LA's actual usage

availability._max_distance_meters_help: Maximum OD distance (meters) for this mode to be available. Walk trips beyond this distance are not generated
Maximum OD distance (meters) for bike trips. Bike trips beyond 8km are not generated

mode_choice._comment: Controls how transportation mode is selected for each trip leg
mode_choice._method_help: Mode choice method. 'survey_rates' = sample from survey-derived mode shares, filtered by availability and adjusted by blend/share settings
mode_choice._fallback_mode_help: Mode to use when no other mode is available for an OD pair (e.g., rural area with no transit and distance too far for walk/bike)
mode_choice._chain_consistency_help: If true, all legs in an activity chain use the same mode (e.g., home→work→home both by car). Mode is sampled once using modes available for ALL legs
mode_choice._min_samples_per_purpose_help: Minimum survey trips needed per trip purpose to compute purpose-specific mode shares. Below this threshold, falls back to overall 'all' rates
mode_choice._max_chain_mode_retries_help: Maximum attempts to find a mode available for all legs in a chain before falling back to fallback_mode

activity_durations._trim_priority_help: Controls trimming order when schedule exceeds 24h. Higher = trimmed first (discretionary before mandatory). 1=mandatory, 2=default, 3=discretionary

duration_constraints._trim_jitter_minutes_help: Random jitter (±minutes) added to trimmed activity durations to prevent departure clustering. 0 = no jitter
duration_constraints._departure_jitter_minutes_help: Random jitter (±minutes) added to first departure time to prevent clustering at KDE peaks. 0 = no jitter

chains._max_length_help: Maximum activities per chain. null = sample from survey chain-length distribution (recommended). Set an integer (e.g. 8) to hard-cap.
chains._min_length_help: Minimum activities per chain. 3 = smallest valid chain (Home-X-Home). Raise to force longer chains.
chains._max_work_activities_help: Maximum Work activities allowed per work chain. 1 = simple commute only, 2 = allows split-shift or lunch-at-home patterns.
chains._early_stop_exponent_help: Controls early stopping aggressiveness in Markov chain generation. 1.0 = linear (original), 2.0 = quadratic (gentler, default), 3.0 = cubic. Higher values produce longer chains.

network._rebuild_network_help: If true, force regeneration of the network even if a cached version exists. Useful when the cached network has issues. After a successful rebuild, set back to false to re-enable caching

matsim._transit_network_help: If true, use pt2matsim pipeline to generate a multimodal network (road + transit) from OSM + GTFS. If false, use the default road-only network generator
matsim._gtfs_sample_day_help: Which day to sample GTFS schedules for. 'dayWithMostTrips' auto-selects the busiest service day. Can also be a specific date string for reproducibility

pt2matsim._comment: Parameters for pt2matsim PublicTransitMapper (Step 3: mapping GTFS schedule to network). These control how transit routes are mapped to network links
pt2matsim._numOfThreads_help: Number of parallel threads for mapping transit routes to the network. Higher = faster for large networks
pt2matsim._candidateDistanceMultiplier_help: When fewer than nLinkThreshold candidate links are found, the search radius is expanded by this multiplier. Higher = more aggressive fallback search in sparse/rural areas
pt2matsim._maxLinkCandidateDistance_help: Maximum distance (meters) to search for candidate network links near a transit stop. Increase for suburban/rural areas where roads are farther from stops
pt2matsim._maxTravelCostFactor_help: Maximum allowed cost ratio (mapped route vs free-speed shortest path) for a route segment. Lower = stricter but forces more artificial links which can cause link-order mismatches. Higher = allows pt2matsim to find real-network paths. Range: 1.5 (very strict) to 5.0 (permissive). Our post-validation catches truly bad routes anyway
pt2matsim._nLinkThreshold_help: Minimum number of candidate links to find within maxLinkCandidateDistance before expanding search by candidateDistanceMultiplier
pt2matsim._travelCostType_help: Cost metric for routing during schedule-to-network mapping. 'linkLength' = use link distance, 'travelTime' = use free-speed travel time
pt2matsim._removeNotUsedStopFacilities_help: Remove stop facilities that could not be mapped to the network. Prevents orphan stops from causing errors
pt2matsim._routingWithCandidateDistance_help: If true, uses candidate-distance-based search for routing (faster). If false, routes on the full network (slower but more thorough)
pt2matsim._scheduleFreespeedModes_help: Transit modes for which schedule-based free-speed is applied during routing. 'artificial' = pt2matsim connector links

counts._rebuild_help: If true, regenerate counts.xml and matched_devices.csv even if they already exist. Set to false to reuse cached files

fha._data_dir_help: Directory containing FHA/TMAS zip archives ({year}_station_data.zip and {mon}_{year}_ccs_data.zip)
fha._month_help: Month of volume data to use (1-12). FHA distributes one zip per month
fha._weight_help: Blending weight for FHA data when both FHA and custom sources match the same network link

custom._enabled_help: If true, look for counts_stations.csv and counts_volumes.csv in the evaluation folder. If files are missing or invalid, falls back to FHA-only with a warning
custom._weight_help: Blending weight for custom data when both sources match the same network link

nonwork_purposes._nonwork_trip_share_help: Share of non-workers who travel on a given day (0.60 = 60%). Applied as a multiplier to all non-work trip categories

trip_generation._comment: Controls what percentage of non-employees make shopping trips per day
trip_generation._survey_rate_help: Trip rate from TBI survey data. Use 'auto' to calculate from survey, or specify a float value (e.g., 0.35 for 35%)
trip_generation._config_rate_help: Target/expected trip rate (e.g., 0.30 means 30% of non-employees make shopping trips)
trip_generation._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending rates: 0.0 = use survey rate only, 1.0 = use config rate only, 0.5 = average both (recommended)

trip_generation._comment: Controls what percentage of non-employees make school trips per day
trip_generation._survey_rate_help: Trip rate from TBI survey data. Use 'auto' to calculate from survey, or specify a float value
trip_generation._config_rate_help: Target/expected trip rate (e.g., 0.20 means 20% of non-employees make school trips)
trip_generation._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending rates: 0.0 = use survey rate only, 1.0 = use config rate only, 0.5 = average both

trip_generation._comment: Controls what percentage of non-employees make social/recreation trips per day
trip_generation._survey_rate_help: Trip rate from TBI survey data. Use 'auto' to calculate from survey, or specify a float value
trip_generation._config_rate_help: Target/expected trip rate (e.g., 0.25 means 25% of non-employees make social/recreation trips)
trip_generation._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending rates: 0.0 = use survey rate only, 1.0 = use config rate only, 0.5 = average both

trip_generation._comment: Controls what percentage of non-employees make dining trips per day
trip_generation._survey_rate_help: Trip rate from TBI survey data. Use 'auto' to calculate from survey, or specify a float value
trip_generation._config_rate_help: Target/expected trip rate (e.g., 0.20 means 20% of non-employees make dining trips)
trip_generation._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending rates: 0.0 = use survey rate only, 1.0 = use config rate only, 0.5 = average both

trip_generation._comment: Controls what percentage of non-employees make other trips per day
trip_generation._survey_rate_help: Trip rate from TBI survey data. Use 'auto' to calculate from survey, or specify a float value
trip_generation._config_rate_help: Target/expected trip rate (e.g., 0.15 means 15% of non-employees make other trips)
trip_generation._blend_weight_help: Weight for blending rates: 0.0 = use survey rate only, 1.0 = use config rate only, 0.5 = average both
